request.question: Does adding 250mM trehalose to freezing medium improve HeLa post-thaw viability vs DMSO?

hypothesis.readiness_rationale: The threshold and mechanistic rationale are missing.
clarifying_questions: What is the numeric success threshold for post-thaw viability?
What is the mechanistic rationale for using trehalose in this context?
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: trehalose freezing medium HeLa post-thaw viability DMSO
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Does adding 250mM trehalose to freezing medium improve HeLa post-thaw viability vs DMSO?

literature_qc.explanation: None of the retrieved papers cover the addition of trehalose to a freezing medium specifically for HeLa cells. While there are papers discussing trehalose in cryopreservation, they either involve different biological systems or do not focus on HeLa cells or the specific outcome of post-thaw viability with DMSO as a control. This results in no exact or similar prior art that matches the hypothesis dimensions effectively.
literature_qc.recommended_action: No directly matching publication found. This combination appears novel. Proceed with experiment planning.

plan.objective: Determine the effect of adding 250 mM trehalose to the freezing medium on the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells compared to DMSO.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be cryopreserved using freezing medium with either 250 mM trehalose or DMSO as a control. The post-thaw viability will be assessed using trypan blue exclusion following a 30-minute recovery at 37°C.
controls: 100% viability HeLa cell culture (non-frozen) for baseline comparison.
Cell viability of DMSO-treated HeLa cells following cryopreservation and thawing.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent preparation
Prepare 250 mL of freezing medium containing 250 mM trehalose in supplemented DMEM with 10% DMSO and 20% FBS at 4°C.
Expected outcome: Freezing medium containing proper concentrations of trehalose, DMSO, and FBS is prepared successfully.
Safety: DMSO is hazardous; wear gloves and goggles.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 24 h | Cell culture
Culture HeLa cells in T-75 flasks in DMEM with 10% FBS at 37°C, 5% CO2 until nearly confluent (approximately 80% confluency).
Expected outcome: HeLa cells reach 80% confluency in 24 h.

DAY 1 | 10 min | Cell harvesting
Trypsinize HeLa cells using 3 mL of 0.25% trypsin-EDTA for 5 min at 37°C to detach cells.
Expected outcome: HeLa cells are successfully detached and in suspension.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Cell counting
Count cells using trypan blue exclusion method: mix 10 µL of cell suspension with 10 µL trypan blue and count live and dead cells under a hemocytometer.
Expected outcome: Cell viability percentage is obtained with >90% accuracy.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Cryopreservation
Aliquot 1 mL of cell suspension (1×10^6 cells/mL) into cryovials for freezing using the prepared freezing medium with trehalose.
Expected outcome: Cells are ready for freezing in trehalose medium.

DAY 1 | 16 h | Cryopreservation
Place cryovials in Mr. Frosty container and store at -80°C for 16 h for gradual cooling at 1°C/min.
Expected outcome: Cryovials are stored under controlled cooling conditions.

DAY 2 | 3 min | Thawing
Thaw cryovials rapidly by placing them in a 37°C water bath until ice melts (~2–3 min).
Expected outcome: Cells are fully thawed and ready for recovery.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Cell recovery
Transfer thawed cells to a 15 mL conical tube containing 9 mL of pre-warmed DMEM with 10% FBS and incubate for 30 min at 37°C.
Expected outcome: Cells are allowed to recover from freezing shock.

DAY 2 | 15 min | Cell counting post-thaw
Count cells using trypan blue exclusion method: mix 10 µL of cell suspension with 10 µL trypan blue and count live and dead cells under a hemocytometer.
Expected outcome: Post-thaw viability percentage is recorded accurately.

DAY 2 | 45 min | Statistical analysis
Perform statistical analysis using a two-tailed t-test comparing the post-thaw viability of trehalose vs. DMSO treated cells.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance of differences in post-thaw viability is determined.

assumptions: Trehalose acts as a cryoprotectant for HeLa cells during freezing and thawing processes.
The post-thaw viability can be reliably assessed using trypan blue exclusion method.
Cells undergo similar recovery processes regardless of the cryopreservation method used.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability of trehalose-treated cells is significantly greater than DMSO, p < 0.05.
Consider methods reproducible if variances in viability across replicates are less than 10%.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same passage number of HeLa cells for all experiments (passage 10-15 recommended).
Standardize DMEM and FBS batches used across experiments.
Ensure all cryovials are sourced from the same manufacturer.
feedback_incorporated:
PRIOR SCIENTIST CORRECTIONS FOR SIMILAR EXPERIMENTS:
A scientist has previously flagged these specific issues on similar comparative_cryopreservation experiments. You MUST implement each correction below in your output unless it directly contradicts the current hypothesis:
1. replicate gate in plan text
2. Add Mr. Frosty step
3. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: >=10 percentage points (pilot fallback) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: Determine the effect of adding 250 mM trehalose to the freezing medium on the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells compared to DMSO.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
